sections.title: What to test
items: Tap the extra word in a sentence
Tap OK when the line is correct
One life lost per wrong screen

exercises.title: F: Find the extra word
exercises.instructions: If a line is correct, click OK. If there is an extra word in a line, click on it.
exercises.studentInstruction: Tap OK if the line is correct, or tap the extra word.

items.sentence: Yesterday our team finally [had] won the school basketball final.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: "had" is the extra word that should be removed from this sentence.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Read the sentence aloud without the tapped word — if it sounds natural, that word is the extra one.

wrongOptions.Yesterday: "Yesterday" is necessary here — the extra word is "had".
wrongOptions.our: "our" is necessary here — the extra word is "had".
wrongOptions.team: "team" is necessary here — the extra word is "had".
wrongOptions.finally: "finally" is necessary here — the extra word is "had".
wrongOptions.had: "had" is the redundant word — tap it (do not tap OK).
wrongOptions.won: "won" is necessary here — the extra word is "had".
wrongOptions.the: "the" is necessary here — the extra word is "had".
wrongOptions.school: "school" is necessary here — the extra word is "had".
wrongOptions.basketball: "basketball" is necessary here — the extra word is "had".
wrongOptions.final: "final" is necessary here — the extra word is "had".
wrongOptions.OK: There is an extra word ("had") — do not tap OK.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Look for a repeated or unnecessary small word — "had" is the one to tap, not a content word the sentence needs.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Try reading the sentence without each word in your head — the extra word is the one that can be dropped with no loss of meaning.

items.sentence: We had [been] trained hard for weeks, so everyone was excited.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: "been" is the extra word that should be removed from this sentence.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Present simple passive uses is/are + past participle — been is not needed after is in this pattern.

wrongOptions.We: "We" is necessary here — the extra word is "been".
wrongOptions.had: "had" is necessary here — the extra word is "been".
wrongOptions.been: "been" is the redundant word — tap it (do not tap OK).
wrongOptions.trained: "trained" is necessary here — the extra word is "been".
wrongOptions.hard: "hard" is necessary here — the extra word is "been".
wrongOptions.for: "for" is necessary here — the extra word is "been".
wrongOptions.weeks: "weeks" is necessary here — the extra word is "been".
wrongOptions.so: "so" is necessary here — the extra word is "been".
wrongOptions.everyone: "everyone" is necessary here — the extra word is "been".
wrongOptions.was: "was" is necessary here — the extra word is "been".
wrongOptions.excited: "excited" is necessary here — the extra word is "been".
wrongOptions.OK: There is an extra word ("been") — do not tap OK.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Look for a repeated or unnecessary small word — "been" is the one to tap, not a content word the sentence needs.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Try reading the sentence without each word in your head — the extra word is the one that can be dropped with no loss of meaning.

items.sentence: The other team scored first, but we didn't give up.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: This sentence has no extra word — tap OK.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: If every word is necessary and the grammar is correct, tap OK — do not remove a word just to change the sentence.

wrongOptions.The: "The" is needed in this sentence — tap OK because there is no extra word.
wrongOptions.other: "other" is needed in this sentence — tap OK because there is no extra word.
wrongOptions.team: "team" is needed in this sentence — tap OK because there is no extra word.
wrongOptions.scored: "scored" is needed in this sentence — tap OK because there is no extra word.
wrongOptions.first: "first" is needed in this sentence — tap OK because there is no extra word.
wrongOptions.but: "but" is needed in this sentence — tap OK because there is no extra word.
wrongOptions.we: "we" is needed in this sentence — tap OK because there is no extra word.
wrongOptions.didn't: "didn't" is needed in this sentence — tap OK because there is no extra word.
wrongOptions.give: "give" is needed in this sentence — tap OK because there is no extra word.
wrongOptions.up: "up" is needed in this sentence — tap OK because there is no extra word.
wrongOptions.OK: There is no extra word to remove — the sentence is already correct.

explanationContent.commonMistake: If the sentence is already correct, tap OK — removing a necessary word would make it wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the whole line first — if grammar and meaning are fine, tap OK without selecting any word.

items.sentence: By half-time, we had [been] scored three points.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: "been" is the extra word that should be removed from this sentence.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Present simple passive uses is/are + past participle — been is not needed after is in this pattern.

wrongOptions.By: "By" is necessary here — the extra word is "been".
wrongOptions.half: "half" is necessary here — the extra word is "been".
wrongOptions.time: "time" is necessary here — the extra word is "been".
wrongOptions.we: "we" is necessary here — the extra word is "been".
wrongOptions.had: "had" is necessary here — the extra word is "been".
wrongOptions.been: "been" is the redundant word — tap it (do not tap OK).
wrongOptions.scored: "scored" is necessary here — the extra word is "been".
wrongOptions.three: "three" is necessary here — the extra word is "been".
wrongOptions.points: "points" is necessary here — the extra word is "been".
wrongOptions.OK: There is an extra word ("been") — do not tap OK.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Look for a repeated or unnecessary small word — "been" is the one to tap, not a content word the sentence needs.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Try reading the sentence without each word in your head — the extra word is the one that can be dropped with no loss of meaning.
